summary: ICSE Crowd is a full-stack Internet Computer (ICP) dapp: a social + academic collaboration platform for ICSE students. The frontend is a React/Tailwind app using Internet Identity for authentication and TanStack React Query for data fetching. The backend is a Motoko canister that provides role-based access control (admin/user/guest), user profiles, a social feed (posts with optional images, likes, comments), basic friend requests, direct messaging, study groups and shared notes, a simple forum posting feature, and a moderation/reporting system (reports, status workflow, admin deletions). The project also includes a Caffeine blob-storage mixin for handling externally stored blobs, gateway authorization, and storage maintenance endpoints.
architectural_notes: Frontend uses React with a global InternetIdentityProvider (AuthClient) context to manage identity, login/logout, and persisted sessions.
TanStack React Query wraps backend calls into hooks (queries/mutations) with cache invalidation keyed by resource (e.g., ['feed'], ['reports']).
An actor factory pattern is used: useActor creates an anonymous actor when unauthenticated, or an authenticated actor with agent identity when logged in.
Backend uses Motoko 'mixin' modules to compose cross-cutting concerns: MixinAuthorization for access control initialization/role checks and MixinStorage for Caffeine blob-storage maintenance APIs.

Role-based access control is implemented in backend/authorization/access-control.mo; initialization uses an admin token from an environment variable and a user-supplied secret to assign the first admin.
Backend data is stored in mo:core/Map maps keyed by Principal or Nat, with simple timestamp-based ID generation.
Frontend UI is built with Tailwind + shadcn-style components, next-themes for dark/light mode, and OKLCH CSS variables for theme tokens.
Sensitive admin token acquisition is designed around URL hash secrets + sessionStorage persistence (frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts), then passed into backend initialization.
Image posts use an ExternalBlob abstraction that supports upload progress callbacks and direct URL rendering on the client.
Project now includes a reusable build-template (React + ICP canister configs, scripts, and UI component baseline) used to scaffold/standardize deployments and frontend tooling.
known_issues: ChatPage uses the admin-only getAllUsers endpoint to populate contacts; non-admin users will trap with "Unauthorized: Only admins can view all users". A user-accessible contacts/friends endpoint is missing.
Friend request acceptance only removes the request; there is no persistent 'friends list' or membership relation created, and there are no endpoints/UI for listing pending requests or friends.
Messaging is write-only from the UI: backend stores chatMessages but there is no query endpoint to fetch conversation history; ChatPage displays only a placeholder conversation view.
Comments can be added but are not retrievable/displayed: backend stores comments, but feed retrieval returns only posts; no getCommentsByPost API exists and UI does not render comments.
ID generation uses Time.now().toNat() and may collide under rapid successive operations (multiple events within the same timestamp granularity).
Backend maps are defined as non-stable in-memory structures; without stable variables/upgrade hooks, data likely will not persist across canister upgrades.
Authentication/login issue: clicking Sign In has a long delay before failing with an authentication error (Internet Identity flow suspected/misconfigured).
Authentication/login issue detail update: Sign In delay occurs every time; after the delay an authentication error message appears consistently.
Username display is still incorrect: posts/comments are not consistently showing the author’s profile display name (set during profile creation).

Study group creation is failing for logged-in users: clicking "Create Study Group" on the Study Groups page produces no UI reaction and no group is created, while other Study Groups page functionality works.

existing_features.title: Internet Identity authentication (login/logout, persisted sessions)
existing_features.description: Provides an InternetIdentityProvider that initializes an AuthClient, loads persisted identity on startup, performs Internet Identity login with a 30-day TTL delegation, and supports logout/clear. Exposes status flags (initializing/logging-in/success/error) to the UI.

existing_features.title: Actor initialization and per-identity backend binding
existing_features.description: Creates an anonymous backend actor when unauthenticated or an identity-bound actor when authenticated, then triggers backend access control initialization via _initializeAccessControlWithSecret. Invalidates/refetches non-actor queries whenever the actor changes.

existing_features.title: Secret admin token extraction from URL hash
existing_features.description: Implements utilities to read secrets from the URL hash fragment, persist them in sessionStorage, and clear them from the URL to reduce leakage. Used to pass caffeineAdminToken into backend access control initialization.

existing_features.title: Role-based access control (admin/user/guest)
existing_features.description: Motoko access control state assigns roles per Principal. The first eligible caller can become admin when providing a matching secret vs CAFFEINE_ADMIN_TOKEN; subsequent users become #user. Includes APIs to query caller role, check admin, and assign roles (admin-only).

existing_features.title: User profile creation and retrieval
existing_features.description: Backend supports createUserProfile, getCallerUserProfile, saveCallerUserProfile, and getUserProfile with role checks. Frontend shows a mandatory ProfileSetupModal for authenticated users without an existing profile.

existing_features.title: Social feed retrieval and rendering
existing_features.description: Backend getFeed returns all posts for users. Frontend FeedPage fetches posts and renders them chronologically (client-sorted by timestamp), including author principal and relative timestamps.

existing_features.title: Create posts with optional image upload and progress
existing_features.description: Users can create text posts and optionally attach an image. The frontend converts a selected file into an ExternalBlob, reports upload progress, and renders uploaded images via getDirectURL().

existing_features.title: Like posts
existing_features.description: Backend likePost adds the caller principal to a post's likes if not already present. Frontend displays like counts and toggles button styling based on whether the current user liked the post.

existing_features.title: Add comments to posts
existing_features.description: Backend addComment stores a comment for a post. Frontend provides per-post comment inputs and submits comments, invalidating the feed on success (though comments are not currently displayed).

existing_features.title: Friend request send/accept (minimal)
existing_features.description: Backend supports sendFriendRequest with blocked-user checks and acceptFriendRequest (removes the pending request). No friend graph or listing endpoints are implemented.

existing_features.title: Direct messaging send (minimal)
existing_features.description: Backend sendMessage stores messages between principals with blocked-user checks. Frontend ChatPage allows selecting a contact and sending a message (conversation history is not implemented).

existing_features.title: Study group creation
existing_features.description: Backend createStudyGroup creates a group with the creator as the initial member. Frontend provides a dialog to create groups (listing is placeholder-only).

existing_features.title: Shared notes posting to study groups (backend)
existing_features.description: Backend addSharedNote allows group members to add shared notes to a study group. A React Query mutation hook exists, but no UI for notes/group membership management is present.

existing_features.title: Forum question/post creation (minimal)
existing_features.description: Backend createForumPost stores forum posts with subject/content. Frontend ForumPage provides a dialog to create posts, but does not fetch/list real forum posts (placeholder cards shown).

existing_features.title: Reporting and user blocking (core APIs)
existing_features.description: Backend supports reporting users/content (creating reports with #pending status) and blocking users (stored per profile). Hooks exist for both operations; reporting review is handled via moderation dashboard.

existing_features.title: Moderation dashboard (admin)
existing_features.description: Frontend ModerationPage fetches reports and users, displays KPIs and tabbed lists by status (pending/reviewed/resolved), and allows admins to update report status. Backend enforces admin-only access to getReports/reviewReport and provides deletePost/deleteComment/deleteForumPost operations.

existing_features.title: Caffeine blob-storage maintenance endpoints
existing_features.description: Backend includes blob-storage mixin providing gateway principal authorization updates, dead blob listing/confirmation for deletion, blob liveness checks, cashier refill logic with cycles transfer, and a stubbed upload certificate creator.

existing_features.title: Responsive themed UI shell with navigation
existing_features.description: App shell includes Header navigation with auth-aware routes and admin-only moderation link, Footer attribution, and theme support (light/dark via next-themes). Styling uses Tailwind with OKLCH token variables and shadcn-style UI components.

existing_features.title: Study groups listing, detail retrieval, and membership management
existing_features.description: Backend now exposes query APIs to list study groups visible to the caller and fetch a single group by id, plus membership management endpoints to join/leave groups. Membership checks are enforced for group-only actions. Frontend Study Groups experience is upgraded from placeholders to render real groups and support join/leave and group detail views.

existing_features.title: Video and document attachments supported via blob storage (in addition to images)
existing_features.description: Backend data model and APIs now support attachments beyond images, including videos and documents using the existing blob storage approach. Attachments include blob reference plus filename and MIME type; content creation flows accept these attachments for feed and study-group content, and the frontend supports selection, upload progress, and rendering (inline video playback and document open/download links).

existing_features.title: Expose a reliable canister API for retrieving comments for a given post ID, and ensure the generated frontend canister bindings include the Comment type and the getPostComments method.

Add a React Query hook to fetch comments for a specific post (keyed by postId), and update comment creation flow to invalidate/refetch the relevant post’s comments after successfully adding a comment.

Render fetched comments in the feed UI: when a user expands a post’s comments section, display a list of comments (author + content + relative timestamp), show a loading indicator while fetching, show an empty-state when there are no comments, and show a readable error message if fetching fails.
Replace principal/placeholder author strings in the feed UI with the author’s profile username for both posts and comments.
Add React Query data access to retrieve a user profile for an arbitrary principal and reuse it to resolve usernames in the feed.
Ensure the frontend prevents posting/commenting when the current user has not created a profile, aligning the UI behavior with the backend enforcement.

Fix the feed UI so that author names for both posts and comments reliably display the profile display name (the name saved at profile creation time) instead of showing principals/placeholders due to principal type/conversion issues.

Update the authenticated Home page to show a full-width (or prominent) "Create Profile" button only when the signed-in user does not have a profile, and hide/disable post creation on Home when the profile is missing.

Implement a new/modified profile-creation flow launched from the Home page Create Profile button (do not navigate to/open an existing separate profile-creation page). Reuse the existing profile fields (name, email, academic details) and call the existing `createUserProfile(name, email, academicDetails)` backend method.

Adjust the app-level gating so users with no profile are not forced into an auto-opening profile modal that blocks the Home page; instead, allow the Home page to render and let the user initiate profile creation via the Home page button.

Update the authenticated Home page so that when the signed-in user does not have a profile, a centered “Create Profile” button is displayed at the very start (top) of the Home page content (above the hero section), while also keeping the existing “Create Profile” button placement(s) elsewhere on the Home page.
Update the Post creation UI to allow posting without a profile: remove the UI block/disable logic that prevents composing and submitting posts when `useGetCallerUserProfile()` returns null.
Update the Comment creation UI to allow commenting without a profile: remove the UI gating that hides/disables the comment input and submission when the current user has no profile.
Display a placeholder author name of "Guest" for any post or comment whose author does not have a user profile available to the frontend (profile lookup returns null).

Fix the Study Groups page so clicking "Create Group" always produces a visible UI response for logged-in users by opening the "Create Study Group" dialog reliably (do not depend on a trigger behavior that can fail when the dialog is controlled via `open` state).

Ensure submitting the "Create Study Group" form provides clear feedback and never fails silently: show a loading state while the create request is in-flight, close the dialog only on success, and show a readable error toast on failure using `normalizeICError`.

After successfully creating a study group, refresh the Study Groups page content so the newly created group is visible without a page reload (invalidate/refetch the study-group list query used by the page).

If study-group listing APIs are missing or incomplete in the backend canister, add/repair a canister query method that returns the current list of study groups needed by the Study Groups page (and ensure generated frontend bindings expose the necessary StudyGroup type and method).

Update the Study Groups frontend page to provide a visible, responsive 'Join Group' option for each study group and show clear feedback (loading/disabled state, success toast, readable error toast) when joining.
Add a Study Group chat UI in the frontend that becomes accessible once a user has joined a study group, allowing members to read the group’s message history and send new messages.
Update the frontend Study Groups page to display a clear, readable error message when study group creation fails due to content moderation

new_feature_requests.summary: Improve study group sessions (enhanced group study features beyond current basic group creation/notes posting)
Fix comments visibility: add backend API to fetch comments per post and wire into feed UI to display comments
Display poster username (profile display name) for both posts and comments; require users to create a profile before they can post or comment
Fix username display: show the profile display name (set at profile creation time) for authors in both posts and comments; current display is still incorrect
Add a full "Create Profile" button on the Home page that appears only when the user has no profile; it should use a new/modified profile-creation flow (not the existing profile-creation page).

Remove the profile requirement for creating posts/comments: allow posting/commenting without a profile on both backend and frontend, and display a placeholder author name (e.g., "Guest") for users without profiles.
Remove the backend profile-existence enforcement for creating posts and adding comments so an authenticated caller can create posts and comments even when they have not created a UserProfile.
Update backend feed access so users without a profile can still retrieve and view the feed (required to support posting/commenting workflows for users without profiles).

Improve backend error messaging for study-group creation when a logged-in caller lacks a profile by returning a study-group-specific message (do not reuse an unrelated message like "User must have a profile to create posts and comments!").
Add a working "Join Study Group" option in the Study Group page/UI so logged-in users can join groups (button visible and responsive, with feedback and membership update).
Add in-group chat so study group members can exchange messages within a study group (group chat tied to membership).

Add backend canister APIs to support Study Group membership changes so a logged-in user can join a study group and the updated membership is persisted and returned by study-group retrieval/listing APIs.
Add backend canister APIs and storage for Study Group chat so that only study group members can send messages to the group and members can fetch the group chat history.
Automatic content moderation for study groups: reject/delete groups with sensitive/banned keywords in description at creation time
Define a predefined list of banned/sensitive keywords in the backend that includes profanity, hate speech, spam terms, and non-academic content indicators
Implement automatic validation in the createStudyGroup backend method that scans the group description for any banned keywords and completely rejects the creation if sensitive words are detected
